codeReview.summary: Manual scrutiny review confirmed the P2P packet addressed the actual PR #98 blockers, and the current propagated stack now keeps that evidence traceable on reachable descendant commit `39b73910e061a42deb23ddc16779b9be726e57f9` (`fix(p2p): close remaining PR #98 live comments`). That current-stack anchor preserves the earlier replay-first supplier settlement/accounting recovery, R2 evidence, docs cleanup, and changed-files coverage closure while also carrying the final PurchaseInvoiceEngine linkage fix that landed later on the same recovery branch.

issues.description: The worker noted that running the full `python3 -m unittest testing.ci.test_pr_review_ci_packet` module still fails `RuntimeProbeContractTest.test_runtime_probe_fails_closed_on_health_status` because `.factory/services.yaml` does not yet match that runtime-probe expectation on this branch.
issues.suggestedFix: Resolve the runtime probe contract in the packet that owns `.factory/services.yaml` healthcheck semantics, then rerun the full CI packet unittest module if that contract becomes part of the required validator surface.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The full CI packet unittest module still contains a runtime-probe contract expectation for a 9090 actuator healthcheck sequence that does not match the current backend-compose-v2 healthcheck in `.factory/services.yaml`, even though the required recovery validators for this packet passed.

summary: Pass with non-blocking follow-up. PR #98 replay/governance/coverage blockers remain validated on branch-reachable commit `39b73910e061a42deb23ddc16779b9be726e57f9`, with the earlier coverage/R2 closure now traced through the current propagated descendant rather than the stale pre-restack commit id.
